title: Taman Balai Kota Bandung
photo_credit: Credit: John Doe, via Wikimedia Commons. Licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0. https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/
description: Located around 1.5 km from the hotel on Jalan Wastukencana, the administrative center of Bandung is rich in history and remarkable architecture. The building was originally a coffee warehouse in 1819 before being converted into a Dutch colonial government office in 1927.

title: Museum of the Asian-African Conference
description:
Located just minutes from Hotel Sukajadi Bandung, the Museum of the Asian–African Conference is one of Bandung’s most iconic heritage sites. Inside its beautifully preserved Art Deco building, visitors can explore the historic 1955 conference that shaped global cooperation between Asia and Africa.

It’s a meaningful stop for tourists who enjoy culture, history, and Bandung’s colonial charm — and staying at Hotel Sukajadi makes it incredibly convenient. You can walk there easily, explore the surrounding Asia Afrika and Braga area, and return to the hotel without needing transport.

title: Braga City Walk
description: Located around 800 meters from Sukajadi Hotel. Braga City Walk is a vibrant shopping and entertainment district featuring a diverse array of local boutiques, international brands, specialty restaurants, and multi-screen cinemas.

title: Paris Van Java Mall
description: Located around 750 meters from Sukajadi Hotel, this premier shopping center offers an excellent blend of luxury shopping, international fashion houses, a massive food market, and entertainment spaces.

title: Gedung Sate
description: The striking neoclassical administrative center of West Java's provincial government, renowned for its heritage museum and its iconic roof ornament styled after a traditional skewered ornament.

title: Kawah Putih (White Crater)
description: A striking sulfurous acid volcanic crater lake located south of Bandung in Ciwidey. The stunning milky-turquoise waters and surrounding skeletal trees offer a mystical landscape perfect for photography.

title: Mount Tangkuban Perahu
description: An iconic active volcano north of the city in Lembang. Visitors can hike or drive right up to the edge of the massive main crater to witness steam vents amidst beautiful mountain backdrops.

title: Saung Angklung Udjo
description: A legendary cultural workshop and performance space dedicated to preserving traditional Sudanese art. Experience live energetic performances featuring the traditional Angklung bamboo instruments.

title: Rumah Mode Factory Outlet
description: Rumah Mode is one of Bandung’s most popular factory outlets, known for its wide selection of fashion at great value. Set in a cozy garden-style complex, it also features a lively food court, cafés, and small entertainment spots, making it a favourite stop for shopping, relaxing, and enjoying local snacks in one place.
